response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development across multiple domains, including embedded systems (National Instruments) and low-latency applications (DLang), but lacks direct experience with embedded networking, security features (e.g., MACsec, IEEE 802.1X), or Linux kernel modifications required for this role. Their strong C/C++ skills are partially offset by limited exposure to network switch operations, chip-vendor SDKs, and telemetry/NETCONF/SNMP. Japanese proficiency is not mentioned in the resume.
strengths: Strong background in C/C++ and embedded systems (National Instruments)
Experience with low-latency and high-performance software (DLang, asynchronous I/O)
Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt to new technologies (multiple languages, frameworks)
concerns: No explicit experience with security features (e.g., MACsec, IEEE 802.1X) or embedded networking
No mention of Japanese language proficiency or exposure to Linux kernel modifications
Limited experience with networking protocols (REST API, NETCONF, SNMP) or switch chip knowledge

key_factors.factor: C programming and Linux kernel knowledge
